message:
fix: fail-closed rate limiter IDs, capability-bound Sleep, oracle + raptorq hardening

Rate limiter (combinator/rate_limit.rs + web/middleware.rs):
- Replace u64::MAX magic number with named IMMEDIATE_ACQUIRE_SENTINEL
- Add allocate_entry_id() that skips the sentinel value and fails closed
  with new QueueIdExhausted error instead of wrapping around (wraparound
  would let stale handles alias later waiters)
- RateLimitMiddleware returns 503 Service Unavailable on QueueIdExhausted
- Tests: queued_entry_ids_fail_closed_when_id_space_exhausts,
  stale_queue_handle_cannot_alias_later_waiter_after_exhaustion

Sleep (time/sleep.rs):
- Add Sleep::with_timer_driver() that permanently binds a Sleep future
  to an explicit TimerDriverHandle, so it works correctly even when
  polled outside the creating Cx's ambient context
- poll() prefers bound_timer_driver over ambient, reads time from it
- Clone preserves the bound driver

ShutdownSignal (server/shutdown.rs):
- wait_until() dispatches to Sleep::with_timer_driver() when the
  captured time source is a TimerDriver, ensuring virtual-clock waits
  work without ambient Cx
- Test: wait_until_uses_captured_timer_driver_without_ambient_context

Http1Listener (http/h1/listener.rs):
- Add shutdown_signal_for_time_getter() that creates ShutdownSignal::new()
  (which captures the ambient timer driver) when using the default time
  getter, instead of always using with_time_getter which bypasses driver
  capture
- Test: default_listener_shutdown_signal_captures_timer_driver

DeadlineMonotoneOracle (lab/oracle/deadline_monotone.rs):
- on_budget_update() now re-checks existing children when a parent's
  deadline tightens, catching violations where a child was registered
  before the parent's budget was reduced
- Test: parent_budget_update_rechecks_existing_children

TaskLeakOracle (lab/oracle/task_leak.rs):
- Extract record_leaked_tasks() helper that deduplicates and merges
  violations for the same region
- on_spawn() now detects post-close spawns immediately (spawning into
  a closed region is itself a structured-concurrency violation)
- Tests: task_spawned_after_region_close_is_violation_even_if_it_
  completes_later, post_close_spawns_merge_into_existing_region_violation

RaptorQ (raptorq/rfc6330.rs):
- Add require_rfc_u32() that panics with a clear message instead of
  silently truncating when systematic_index, lt_width, or pi_modulus
  exceed u32::MAX on 64-bit platforms
- Tests: tuple_rejects_oversized_width_before_u32_truncation,
  tuple_rejects_oversized_systematic_index_before_u32_truncation

Geodesic trace (trace/geodesic.rs):
- Gate 9 decision-ledger tests with #[cfg(feature = "test-internals")]

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
